items.title: Europe swelters under record heat
items.description: Tens of millions of people are enduring extreme temperatures across Europe as a deadly heatwave pushes eastward. Scientists say a heatwave of this intensity so early in the summer would have been virtually impossible without human-caused climate change. Switzerland has broken its record for the hottest June day for the third consecutive day, while two nuclear reactors were taken offline after the Aare River became too warm to cool them. Denmark also recorded its highest temperature on record on Saturday, and Spain has reported 327 heat-related deaths this week. As Europe's fastest-warming continent faces increasingly severe heat, Nick Rushworth has the roundup.

items.title: Iran, US trade attacks in the Strait of Hormuz: Navigating the 'Guardian Angel' route
items.description: Shots have been fired in the Strait of Hormuz. The is a diplomatic and logistical condundrum of what to do next. A literal rock and a hard place, where 11,000 crew remain stuck on ships in the middle of two warring belligerents now negotiating peace, telling them opposing advice on how they can safely evacuate. The Iranian regime is telling them to leave, but to first cross via one of its islands Larak Island at the northern part of the Strait. France 24's Gavin Lee spoke to Nitya Labh, Academy Fellow, International Security Programme at Chatham House.

items.description: Despite a fragile ceasefire, tensions between the United States and Iran remain high, with both sides accusing each other of violating the truce. Tehran says it has carried out a third consecutive day of retaliatory strikes against US military sites in the Middle East, while Washington has warned that any renewed escalation could trigger overwhelming retaliation. President Donald Trump has threatened to "annihilate" Iran if full-scale conflict resumes, and Iranian officials have responded by saying they are prepared to abandon peace negotiations altogether, raising fears that the ceasefire could quickly collapse into a wider war. Charlotte Lam reports.

items.description: Argentina's all-time World Cup top scorer has been in unstoppable form, netting all five of his side's goals in two opening victories. As he celebrates his 39th birthday, are we finally coming to the end of the star's career? Leer más

items.title: Defence seeks acquittal for former naval commander in ARA San Juan submarine trial
items.description: Prosecutors on Monday urged a five-year prison sentence for Claudio Villamide, commander of the submarine force at the time of the tragedy; Defence lawyers request acquittal, arguing there is no evidence linking his actions to the deaths of the crew Leer más

items.title: There's a beef about beef at the World Cup, as Argentina fans pour into Texas
items.description: It's about who produces the best, most succulent steaks, and how to prepare the meat. Argentina and Texas are two of the top cattle-raising areas of the world, where steak is deeply ingrained in diet and culture.

items.title: Banning social media for teens: Solution or overreaction?
items.description: One in seven teens in the European Union spends more than eight hours a day in front of screens, a new study shows. Are social media bans the answer?

items.title: Coach Hugo Broos hails unity behind South Africa’s World Cup surge
items.description: Broos's side now stands just one victory away from an unprecedented place in the last 16, but the veteran Belgian believes the strong bond within the squad has been the foundation of South Africa's success. items.title: UN Report accuses Israel of deliberately targeting children in Gaza
items.description: The report also details alleged abuses against Palestinian children in the occupied West Bank, including arbitrary detention, torture and sexual violence.

items.title: Nigeria's Senate passes bill to allow creation of state police
items.description: The Nigerian Senate on Wednesday approved a bill to allow states to have their own police forces, paving the way for a major reform that would reshape the policing structure of the conflict-racked nation.

items.title: In pictures: Race against time to rescue Venezuela earthquake survivors
items.description: Time is running out in Venezuela's race to locate survivors as experts highlight the crucial 72-hour rescue window.

items.title: Seoul says Chinese, Russian military aircraft enter its air defence zone
items.description: The Joint Chiefs of Staff in Seoul said the Chinese and Russian aircraft had entered and then left the Korea Air Defence Identification Zone over East Sea and South Sea

items.title: Ex-Trump adviser John Bolton pleads guilty to mishandling classified documents
items.description: Bolton faces a prison sentence of up to five years and has agreed to pay $2.25m in fine, prosecutors say.

items.title: Fact check: As Europe bakes, should you trust your sunscreen?
items.description: As a heatwave sweeps across Europe, claims are resurfacing online questioning whether sunscreen is safe or linked to skin cancer risk.

items.title: South Africa inmates embrace art to curb repeat offending
items.description: At a correctional facility in Johannesburg, South Africa’s largest city, visitors are greeted by something unexpected: an art gallery. The exhibition of inmates' artwork is part of the country’s effort to reduce repeat offending through rehabilitation programs behind bars.

items.title: INDEC says Argentina's unemployment rate stood at 7.8% in first quarter
items.description: According to INDEC, 1.1 million people are out of work and searching for jobs; Those in employment represent 44.8% of the population, equivalent to 13.5 million people. Leer más

items.title: Frozen feasts and cool pools help Rome zoo animals survive heatwave
items.description: As a severe heatwave grips Italy, Rome's Bioparco is helping its animals stay cool with frozen treats, chilled pools and climate-controlled shelters while the capital remains under a red heat alert.

items.title: Solidarity rally in Ohio after court ends protected status for Haitians
items.description: Residents gathered Thursday in Ohio in support of Haitian migrants following a Supreme Court decision to allow the Trump administration to end legal protections for migrants from Haiti and Syria fleeing violence and natural disaster in their country

items.title: Iraq: Ashoura in Karbala draws thousands of Shiite pilgrims for rituals
items.description: Thousands of Shiite pilgrims mark Ashoura in Karbala, mourning Imam Hussein’s 680 AD death with processions, prayers and reinforced security.

items.title: Milei’s ‘Super RIGI’ investment bill approved by lower house

items.description: South Africa's president has warned anti-immigration groups against trying to impose the so-called June 30th deadline for illegal migrants to leave the country.

items.title: How countries live with earthquakes and build resilience
items.description: Earthquakes are a fact of nature, but living with earthquakes doesn't have to mean living in fear. DW takes a closer look at some countries showing how preparation, innovation and determination can save lives.

items.title: Comedy legend Mel Brooks turns 100
items.description: A parody of Adolf Hitler in the landmark 1968 movie "The Producers" was just the beginning for US actor Mel Brooks. The 100-year-old comedian is still on a roll today.

items.title: Venezuela: Survivors say rescue effort too slow
items.description: Nearly 7 million people may have been affected by the twin earthquakes that struck Venezuela this week, the United Nations said on Saturday, as the death toll approached 1,000 and tens of thousands remained missing. The UN said up to 6.76 million people could have been impacted, including around two million residents of the capital, Caracas. Shirli Sitbon reports.

items.title: Families calling out to loved ones trapped in rubble by Venezuela quakes
items.description: In La Guaira, desperate families keep vigil at buildings where they fear their relatives are trapped, but face an impossible task to move heavy debris.

items.title: Why Germans don't have air conditioning
items.description: Unlike the US or parts of Asia, many homes in Germany and northern Europe aren't equipped to deal with extreme heat. But as the world gets hotter, trends are changing.

items.title: US and Iran exchange strikes and accuse each other of violating ceasefire
items.description: Iran says it has launched retaliatory attacks at US infrastructure in Kuwait and Bahrain after the US said it hit multiple targets across Iran.

items.title: PM Modi, Trump share similar views on illegal immigration: U.S. Ambassador Gor
items.description: “When I listen to the Prime Minister speaking in India, he talks about no illegal migrants. We hundred per cent agree with that,” U.S. Ambassador to India Sergio Gor said

items.title: Venezuela earthquakes: bodies recovered as rescue efforts continue in La Guaira
items.description: Rescue teams worked through the ruins of collapsed buildings in La Guaira on 25 June after twin earthquakes devastated northern Venezuela, killing at least 188 people and injuring around 1,500.

items.title: Six terrorists killed, one captured after Rangers’ base attack in Pakistan’s Karachi
items.description: Four soldiers dead; attack claimed by Jamaat-ul-Ahrar, a militant faction of the TTP that has largely operated in Khyber Pakhtunkhwa province bordering Afghanistan

items.title: Prominent Tunisian rights activist Sihem Bensedrine sentenced to 25 years
items.description: Once hailed as a champion of Tunisia's post-Arab Spring transition, rights activist **Sihem Bensedrine** now faces 25 years in prison. The former head of the Truth and Dignity Commission has been sentenced on charges that include falsifying part of the commission's final report.

items.title: Messi in Dallas, messy down here
items.description: The separation of powers is alive and well in Argentina when it comes to corruption – regardless of whether the issue is Cabinet Chief Manuel Adorni’s assets or the up to US$20 million stacked in the San Vicente mansion, the knee-jerk response of the political world is: “The matter belongs to the courts.” Leer más

items.title: Three Lesotho nationals charged with murder after mass shooting in South Africa
items.description: The three suspects appeared in a Johannesburg court on Thursday for the mass shooting in which 13 people died and 14 others were injured.

items.title: Right sweeps Latin America with new victory in Colombia
items.description: The right has won elections from Argentina to Honduras. But experts see little evidence of a structural ideological shift. Instead, they point to a political landscape that favours anti-establishment outsider candidates. Leer más
